proposedAction: Create or connect one dedicated Netlify staging-only site for Social Media Management System v1 and deploy from main.
preExecutionChecks: Confirm active approval lock approval-20260704-social-media-netlify-staging exists and is unexpired.
Run npm.cmd run validate in AG OS.
Run npm.cmd run boot:check in AG OS.
Run node --test tests/*.test.mjs in AG OS.
Confirm target main SHA is d3fcd8c6435433169686c2ec404a5f00c6cc62bd or stop for renewed approval.
Confirm no environment variables are required for this static staging site.
Confirm Netlify action is staging-only and no custom domain or DNS change is requested.
Confirm no social account connection, social credential, posting, scheduling, analytics API use, or n8n activation is requested.
validationPlan: Use target repo static files from main.
Use Netlify publish directory root because index.html is at repository root.
Do not add netlify.toml unless a later PR explicitly approves repository changes.
Do not add deploy hooks, tracking scripts, analytics, forms, social OAuth, posting, scheduling, n8n activation, or environment variables.
After any future approved deploy, verify the staging URL returns HTTP 200.
After any future approved deploy, record Netlify deploy result, URL, deploy id if available, and cost ledger entry in AG OS.
rollbackPlan: If the staging deploy is created successfully but should not remain public, stop and ask the owner before deleting or disabling the Netlify site unless immediate exposure risk exists.
If the wrong repository, branch, or commit is deployed, stop all further action, record an incident/audit event, and do not redeploy until owner approval.
If a custom domain, DNS, paid feature, credential, social account connection, posting, scheduling, analytics API use, n8n activation, or real client data becomes necessary, stop before action.
If rollback is approved, use Netlify rollback or site disable/delete path appropriate to the actual result and record the action in AG OS.
stopConditions: No active approval lock for this exact Netlify staging action.
Target repository, branch, or main SHA differs from this plan.
Netlify asks for a paid feature, custom domain, production deploy, DNS change, or credential storage in source control.
Deploy would expose real client data, customer data, production data, tracking, analytics, live forms, social credentials, social account connection, posting, scheduling, or private endpoints.
Validation, boot check, runtime tests, or target repo checks fail.
Any action would activate n8n or touch Lead Gen, AI Receptionist, or the Constitution.
